input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the rear wheel package on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the rear wheel package on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. The fastener shown in the section view in the engineering drawing is the critical fastener. It has 1 thread/mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.8.2.4 specified in the FSAE rule document? To answer the question, use the scale bar shown at the top of the engineering drawing to compute necessary dimensions in the drawing.  First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.8.2.4
            TITLE: a minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.8.2.4: A minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.
T.8.2.4 A minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule T.8.2.4 requires that a minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut. The engineering drawing, specifically the cross-section view labeled DETAIL A, depicts the critical fastener assembly. This view clearly shows threads projecting from the lock nut. Based on the visual representation in the technical drawing, the threads project a length that appears to meet or exceed the minimum required length of two full threads.

Answer: Yes
